title: fix: validate synchronous D1 Tinybird replacement
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Treats multipart `mode=replace` uploads as synchronous Tinybird operations.
- Removes the invalid `/v0/jobs/<datasource-id>` polling that made all five jobs fail after successful replacement in [run 29250993475](https://github.com/pollinations/pollinations/actions/runs/29250993475).
- Validates imported, quarantined, and invalid counts directly from the replacement response without logging response bodies.
- Production verification found every exported row present across all five datasources with zero quarantine rows.
- Validated with actionlint, embedded Bash syntax checking, and `git diff --check`.

gist.headline: Five jobs, zero ghosts
gist.blurb: The D1-to-Tinybird garden now trusts a completed replacement when it sees one. Five datasources made the trip with every row accounted for and no phantom job polling.
gist.summary: Updated the D1 Tinybird sync workflow so multipart mode=replace uploads are handled as synchronous operations, validating imported, quarantined, and invalid row counts directly from the replacement response instead of polling invalid /v0/jobs/<datasource-id> endpoints.
gist.impact: Production data syncs can now complete cleanly across all five datasources, with exported rows verified present and zero quarantine rows.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy data-garden migration: Polly, wearing a tiny mechanic tool belt, tends five small lime-green data pipes flowing from D1-labeled storage pots into a Tinybird-shaped pixel birdhouse. Each pipe ends in a neat completed checkmark, representing synchronous replacement responses and direct row-count validation; no abandoned polling terminal is needed. Small flowers and code-plants make the infrastructure feel cared for rather than dramatic.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush and light blue wings, wearing a tiny mechanic tool belt while checking five glowing lime green (#ecf874) data pipes flowing from little database pots into a friendly pixel birdhouse terminal labeled “TINYBIRD”; each pipe has a completed checkmark and code-flowers grow nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley, with subtle CRT glow and a warm cream background.
